EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rains occurred over the northwest sections of Puerto Rico causing urban and small stream flooding.  Small streams in Camuy  and San Sebastian went out of their banks. A car with his occupant was carried away by a small stream in Camuy but he was later rescued unharmed.

Heavy rains occurred across the southwest sections of Puerto Rico.  The municipalities most affected were Penuelas and Mayaguez with urban flooding, landslides and blocked roads.  Also, a small stream in Mayaguez went out of its banks and carried away a car with his occupant. He was later rescued unharmed.

Heavy rains occurred across the southwest sections of Puerto Rico.  The municipalities most affected were Penuelas and Mayaguez with urban flooding, landslides and blocked roads.  Also, a small stream in Mayaguez went out of its banks and carried away a car with his occupant. He was later rescued unharmed.
Lightning struck a house in Penuelas causing some structural damage and injuring a child when a light bulb exploded.

Heavy rains occurred over the northwest sections of Puerto Rico causing urban and small stream flooding.  Small streams in Camuy  and San Sebastian went out of their banks. A car with his occupant was carried away by a small stream in Camuy but he was later rescued unharmed.

Torrential rains during the afternoon hours over the northwest sections of Puerto Rico caused The Culebrinas River to overflow its banks. The municipalities affected were: San Sebastian, Moca, Aguada and Aguadilla,  where several roads, low lying areas and about 100 residences were affected by the waters.

The municipalities affected were: San Sebastian, Moca, Aguada and Aguadilla,  where several roads, low lying areas and about 100 residences were affected by the waters.

Torrential rains during the afternoon hours over the northwest sections of Puerto Rico caused The Culebrinas River to overflow its banks. The municipalities affected were: San Sebastian, Moca, Aguada and Aguadilla,  where several roads, low lying areas and about 100 residences were affected by the waters.
High seas overturned a fisherman's boat at the Vega Alta Coast.  One of the occupants drowned while the other received injuries as he hit the reef.
General public reported pea size hail in Rio Piedras sector in San Juan and in Guaynabo.
A weather spotter sighted two funnel clouds off the coast of Hatillo.
Three people were on the reef away from the shore when large breaking waves swept them away.  A 16 year old man drowned while the others were rescued.

Heavy rains caused flash flooding over parts of Vega Alta, Vega Baja and Dorado.  The Cibuco river in Vega Baja went out of its banks flooding several roads and low lying areas. Several houses were affected by the waters.
Heavy rains developed in the afternoon over the west sections of Puerto Rico. This rainfall caused urban flooding in the municipality of San German and affected three residences.

An upper level trough was nearly stationary across the local area. This system produced periods of heavy rains for most of the day across the east sections of Puerto Rico. This rainfall caused landslides and several rivers to overflow theirs banks inundating adjacent areas. Reports from Emergency Manager Officials indicated that several houses in Humacao were affected by the waters, various cars stalled in flooded roads in Naguabo and a bridge in Las Piedras collapsed.

Heavy rains caused various roads and a sector in Naguabo to flood.

Torrential rains occurred over the northwest sections of Puerto Rico but mainly in the vicinity of Isabela.  Rainfall accumulation was near six inches of rain in a three hour period. This rainfall caused many irrigation channels to overflow affecting several roads and communities in low lying areas.  Emergency Managers  indicated that about 100 houses were affected by the waters as well as some factories and small businesses along road 2.  In addition,  crops of beans, coriander and eggplant were affected by the waters.
Heavy rains occurred once again in the vicinity of Isabela due to daytime heating and local effects causing several roads to flood and some communities affected by water.

A stationary upper level trough in the vicinity of the area caused heavy rains over the northeast sections of Puerto Rico. This rainfall caused several landslides in Luquillo where four families were moved to safety after their houses were partially destroyed. Other low lying areas in Luquillo were flooded.
Heavy rains caused a bridge to collapse in a sector in Barranquitas and various landslides.
Heavy rains caused a small stream to overflow its banks in Utuado flooding four houses nearby and low lying areas. In addition, this rainfall caused various landslides.
Heavy rains caused landslides and various roads to flood in Bayamon affecting some residences.  In addition, urban flooding was reported in Toa Alta.

A surface trough was located across the local region producing a south and moist wind flow.  Numerous showers and thunderstorms persisted over the south sections of Puerto Rico for most of the day causing landslides and flooded roads.   Hundreds of residents in the coastal sectors in Juana Diaz were evacuated as the floodgates were opened north of the town.  A landslide blocked traffic in Juana Diaz and another landslide in Guayanilla caused a tree to topple over electric lines.  In Ponce, a water canal overflow  its banks flooding a major highway.

Strong thunderstorms occurred in the vicinity of Moca and Aguadilla.  These storms produced small hail and strong gusty winds that knocked down tree branches over  power lines,  palm fronds and street  signs.

Strong thunderstorms occurred in the vicinity of Manati, San Sebastian and Moca. These storms produced small hail and strong gusty winds that knocked down tree branches and electric wires.

Strong thunderstorms occurred in the vicinity of Manati, San Sebastian and Moca. These storms produced small hail and strong gusty winds that knocked down tree branches and electric wires.
Lightning struck a utility pole and knocked it  down at Culebrinas sector in San Sebastian.
A Private Meteorologist and several people spotted a few funnel clouds in the vicinity of Rio Piedras sector and Guaynabo.
Strong thunderstorms caused very heavy rain over the north central sections of Puerto Rico.  Emergency Manager Officials indicated a tree down in Toa Alta and a mudslide in Corozal.

A strong tropical wave caused very heavy rain and strong gusty winds across the northeast sections of Puerto Rico.  The hardest hit  municipalities were Loiza,  Canovanas and Rio Grande.  In Loiza, about 300 homes were affected by flooding,  in Canovanas were about 26 homes and 22 families in Rio Grande.  In addition, around 15,000 Puerto Rico Electric Power Authority subscribers were without power.   Flooding of streets and low and poor drainage areas occurred across most of northeast Puerto Rico.

Hail of unknown size was reported in Cidra.
A spotter sighted a funnel cloud in San Sebastian.
A spotter sighted a funnel cloud near Moca.
Heavy rains caused a stream to overflow its banks covering a bridge in San Sebastian.
Personnel of Ramey Solar Observatory sighted a funnel cloud in the vicinity of Rincon.
Various roads and avenues in the San Juan metropolitan area flooded due to heavy rains.
Lightning struck a residence in Moca causing roof, electrical cables and antenna damages in his residence.
Lightning struck a gas cylinder in a cock fight arena in Anasco causing an explosion and fire damaging the structure.
Heavy rains caused urban flooding over parts of the San Juan Metropolitan Area.  Some main avenues were flooded.
Heavy rains caused a small stream to overflow its banks in Mayaguez. The waters went over a bridge inundating a nearby road.
Aguadilla Airport Observer sighted a funnel cloud south of the airport.
Heavy rains caused a river to overflow its banks in San Sebastian.  A car and its occupants were swept away by the waters, but  they were later rescued unharmed.
Rio Culebrinas in Aguadilla went out of its banks flooding a nearby road.
Heavy rains caused a small stream in Coamo to overflow a bridge.
Emergency Managers reported that a resident of Aguada, Malpaso sector, sighted a tornado.  A house was affected as the zinc roof was detached from the rest of the house.

Heavy rains occurred over the north central sections of  Puerto Rico. The affected municipalities were Arecibo, where power lines and tree branches were on the ground, and  Morovis with one house flooded.

Heavy rains occurred over the north central sections of  Puerto Rico. The affected municipalities were Arecibo, where power lines and tree branches were on the ground, and  Morovis with one house flooded.
Heavy rains caused urban flooding in parts of Arecibo.  Six houses were flooded with three feet of water.
Heavy rains caused a small stream to overflow its banks.  A car was trapped underneath a bridge.
Heavy rains caused urban flooding in various sectors of the San Juan metropolitan area.  Various roads and a house in Puerto Nuevo were flooded.

A tropical depression well to the south of Puerto Rico caused heavy rains and strong gusty winds across the island. The areas most affected were Cayey and Cidra where various homes were affected by the waters and landslides. In addition, in Yauco, a  landslide collapsed a fence falling over power lines and in Rincon strong winds affected power and phone lines.
A tropical wave caused heavy rains and strong gusty winds over parts of eastern Puerto Rico and the island of Vieques.  Several roads were flooded in Fajardo and Vieques.
A tropical wave caused heavy rains and strong gusty winds over parts of eastern Puerto Rico and the island of Vieques.  Several roads were flooded in Fajardo and Vieques.
Heavy rains caused several roads to flood in downtown Mayaguez and urban flooding in Guanajibo sector.

A volcanic eruption occurred over the island of Montserrat in the Lesser Antilles, southeast of the local region.  Southeast winds carried a thin volcanic ash cloud toward the eastern sections of Puerto Rico during the morning hours.  This cloud moved northwest away  from the area in the late afternoon and dissipated.

Strong thunderstorms developed during the afternoon hours over the western sections of Puerto Rico. The municipalities most affected with heavy rain were Lares and Utuado where various landslides were observed blocking the roads.

Strong thunderstorms occurred during the afternoon across the northern sections of Puerto Rico.  The areas most affected were San Juan, Trujillo Alto and Vega Alta where small hail was observed. Strong gusty winds knocked down tree branches in Rio Piedras sector in San Juan.

Heavy rains occurred in the San Juan metropolitan area flooding main avenues. This rainfall caused massive traffic jams.
Lightning struck a power pole, knocking down power lines at the intersection of roads 853 and 859.
Main roads in Levittown sector flooded due to heavy rains.
